description: Recto: Bottom part of a letter from Berakhot probably to Avraham Maimonides (r. 1205–37). Concerning public affairs. Asking him to act with regard to certain dangers arising from the gathering of young (Jewish) men and from leasing houses in the Jewish quarter to Muslims. People involved: a kohl seller (al-kaḥḥāl) (who has been allowing the young men to gather at his place); Ibn Rummān and his mother (they are the ones with Muslim tenants); and the father of the kaḥḥāl, who is Barakāt al-Ṣīrī. Greetings to the sons Moshe and ʿOvadya.
